Identifier for the container used in this request

LocalDateTime expiresAtÓāö

The time at which the container will expire.

ÓÄ™

Optional<List<[BetaSkill](api/beta.md)>> skillsÓāö

Skills loaded in the container

String skillIdÓāö

Skill ID

ÓÄ™

Type typeÓāö

Type of skill - either 'anthropic' (built-in) or 'custom' (user-defined)

One of the following:

ANTHROPIC("anthropic")Óāö

CUSTOM("custom")Óāö

String versionÓāö

Skill version or 'latest' for most recent version

ÓÄ™

List<[BetaContentBlock](api/beta.md)> contentÓāö

Content generated by the model.

This is an array of content blocks, each of which has a `type` that determines its shape.

Example:

```shiki
[{"type": "text", "text": "Hi, I'm Claude."}]
```

ÓĀĖ

If the request input `messages` ended with an `assistant` turn, then the response `content` will continue directly from that last turn. You can use this to constrain the model's output.

For example, if the input `messages` were:

```shiki
[
  {"role": "user", "content": "What's the Greek name for Sun? (A) Sol (B) Helios (C) Sun"},
  {"role": "assistant", "content": "The best answer is ("}
]
```

ÓĀĖ

Then the response `content` might be:

```shiki
[{"type": "text", "text": "B)"}]
```

ÓĀĖ

One of the following:

ÓÄ™

class BetaTextBlock:Óāö

ÓÄ™

Optional<List<[BetaTextCitation](api/beta.md)>> citationsÓāö

Citations supporting the text block.

The type of citation returned will depend on the type of document being cited. Citing a PDF results in `page_location`, plain text results in `char_location`, and content document results in `content_block_location`.

One of the following:

ÓÄ™

long documentIndexÓāö

long endPageNumberÓāö

long startPageNumberÓāö

ÓÄ™

class BetaCitationContentBlockLocation:Óāö

ÓÄ™

String citedTextÓāö

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

long documentIndexÓāö

Optional<String> documentTitleÓāö

ÓÄ™

long endBlockIndexÓāö

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

Optional<String> fileIdÓāö

long startBlockIndexÓāö

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

JsonValue; type "content\_block\_location"constantÓāö"content\_block\_location"constant

ÓÄ™

long endBlockIndexÓāö

Optional<List<[BetaTextCitation](api/beta.md)>> citationsÓāö

Citations supporting the text block.

The type of citation returned will depend on the type of document being cited. Citing a PDF results in `page_location`, plain text results in `char_location`, and content document results in `content_block_location`.

One of the following:

ÓÄ™

class BetaCitationCharLocation:Óāö

String citedTextÓāö

long documentIndexÓāö

long documentIndexÓāö

Optional<String> documentTitleÓāö

long endPageNumberÓāö

Optional<String> fileIdÓāö

long startPageNumberÓāö

JsonValue; type "page\_location"constantÓāö"page\_location"constant

ÓÄ™

class BetaCitationContentBlockLocation:Óāö

ÓÄ™

String citedTextÓāö

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

long documentIndexÓāö

Optional<String> documentTitleÓāö

ÓÄ™

long endBlockIndexÓāö

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

Optional<String> fileIdÓāö

long startBlockIndexÓāö

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

JsonValue; type "content\_block\_location"constantÓāö"content\_block\_location"constant

ÓÄ™

String encryptedIndexÓāö

String urlÓāö

ÓÄ™

long endBlockIndexÓāö

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

ÓÄ™

long searchResultIndexÓāö

0-based index of the cited search result among all `search_result` content blocks in the request, in the order they appear across messages and tool results.

Counted separately from `document_index`; server-side web search results are not included in this count.

minimum0

long startBlockIndexÓāö

class BetaCompactionBlock:Óāö

A compaction block returned when autocompact is triggered.

When content is None, it indicates the compaction failed to produce a valid
summary (e.g., malformed output from the model). Clients may round-trip
compaction blocks with null content; the server treats them as no-ops.

Optional<String> contentÓāö

Summary of compacted content, or null if compaction failed

Optional<String> encryptedContentÓāö

Opaque metadata from prior compaction, to be round-tripped verbatim

JsonValue; type "compaction"constantÓāö"compaction"constant

ÓÄ™

class BetaFallbackBlock:Óāö

Marks the point in `content` where one model's output gives way to the next.

One block appears per hop where a preceding model actually ran this turn and
declined. A turn routed directly by the sticky decision has no such boundary
and carries no block ‚ÄĒ the signal for whether a fallback model served the
response is the presence of a `fallback_message` entry in
`usage.iterations`, not this block.

The block is treated like a server-tool content block for streaming: it
arrives via the standard `content_block_start` / `content_block_stop`
pair and carries no deltas.

ÓÄ™

[BetaFallbackInfo](api/beta.md) fromÓāö

The model whose output ends at this point ‚ÄĒ the model that declined at this hop. When the declining hop is the requested model, its `model` echoes the top-level `model` string the caller sent (alias or canonical); when the declining hop is a fallback model, its `model` is that model's canonical id.

ÓÄ™

Model modelÓāö

The model that will complete your prompt.

See [models](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/models-overview) for additional details and options.

One of the following:

CLAUDE\_FABLE\_5("claude-fable-5")Óāö

Next generation of intelligence for the hardest knowledge work and coding problems

CLAUDE\_MYTHOS\_5("claude-mythos-5")Óāö

Most capable model for cybersecurity and biology research

CLAUDE\_OPUS\_4\_8("claude-opus-4-8")Óāö

Frontier intelligence for long-running agents and coding

CLAUDE\_OPUS\_4\_7("claude-opus-4-7")Óāö

Frontier intelligence for long-running agents and coding

Best combination of speed and intelligence

Fastest model with near-frontier intelligence

Optional<[BetaDiagnostics](api/beta.md)> diagnosticsÓāö

Response envelope for request-level diagnostics. Present (possibly
null) whenever the caller supplied `diagnostics` on the request.

Optional<CacheMissReason> cacheMissReasonÓāö

Explains why the prompt cache could not fully reuse the prefix from the request identified by `diagnostics.previous_message_id`. `null` means diagnosis is still pending ‚ÄĒ the response was serialized before the background comparison completed.

One of the following:

ÓÄ™

ÓÄ™

JsonValue; role "assistant"constantÓāö"assistant"constant

Conversational role of the generated message.

This will always be `"assistant"`.

ÓÄ™

Optional<[BetaRefusalStopDetails](api/beta.md)> stopDetailsÓāö

Structured information about a refusal.

ÓÄ™

Optional<Category> categoryÓāö

The policy category that triggered the refusal.

`null` when the refusal doesn't map to a named category.

One of the following:

CYBER("cyber")Óāö

BIO("bio")Óāö

REASONING\_EXTRACTION("reasoning\_extraction")Óāö

ÓÄ™

Optional<String> explanationÓāö

Human-readable explanation of the refusal.

This text is not guaranteed to be stable. `null` when no explanation is available for the category.

ÓÄ™

Optional<String> fallbackCreditTokenÓāö

Opaque code that refunds the cache-miss cost when retrying this refused
request on the fallback model. Pass it as `fallback_credit_token` on the
retry request. Expires 5 minutes after the refusal.

The retry is sent either with the same request body (`system`, `messages`,
`tools`, and other render-shaping fields), or with the same body plus one
appended `assistant` message whose content is the partial text (with any
trailing whitespace stripped from the final text block) and paired
server-tool blocks from this refusal ‚ÄĒ which also authorizes that
appended turn as an assistant-prefill continuation on models that otherwise
disallow prefill. A token minted mid-server-tool-loop whose partial content
was continuable may only be redeemed the second way ‚ÄĒ if a same-body retry
is rejected with a 400 saying the token must be redeemed by continuing the
partial response, retry the second way instead. Either way: same workspace,
same platform; a mismatch is a 400. Resending a token for an already-warm
prefix is permitted but yields no additional credit.

`null` when the refused model isn't eligible for a fallback credit.

ÓÄ™

Optional<Boolean> fallbackHasPrefillClaimÓāö

Whether the accompanying `fallback_credit_token` may be redeemed with the
appended-assistant retry form. Only set when `fallback_credit_token` is
present.

`true`: retry by resending the same request body plus one appended
`assistant` message whose content is this response's `content` with any
trailing whitespace stripped from the final text block and unpaired
`tool_use` blocks omitted (the same appended-turn shape described on
`fallback_credit_token`), with the token attached. `false`: retry by
resending the original request body unchanged, with the token attached ‚ÄĒ
the appended-assistant form is not available for this refusal (no
continuable partial content, or the request uses `output_format` or a
`tool_choice` that forces tool use). One exception: when the request used
`output_format` or a forced `tool_choice` and the refusal arrived after
server tools (including MCP connector tools) had already executed, the
token may not be redeemable by either retry form; if the exact-body retry
is then rejected with a 400 saying the token must be redeemed by
continuing the partial response, discard the token and retry without it.

Advisory: if an appended-assistant retry is rejected with a 400 despite
`true`, fall back to resending the original request body with the token.

Optional<String> recommendedModelÓāö

The server's suggested retry target for this refusal. Populated when a fallback attempt could not be made (the fallback model's rate limit was exhausted, or it was overloaded); names the fallback model the caller can retry directly. Null otherwise.

JsonValue; type "refusal"constantÓāö"refusal"constant

ÓÄ™

Optional<[BetaStopReason](api/beta.md)> stopReasonÓāö

The reason that we stopped.

This may be one the following values:

- `"end_turn"`: the model reached a natural stopping point
- `"max_tokens"`: we exceeded the requested `max_tokens` or the model's maximum
- `"stop_sequence"`: one of your provided custom `stop_sequences` was generated
- `"tool_use"`: the model invoked one or more tools
- `"pause_turn"`: we paused a long-running turn. You may provide the response back as-is in a subsequent request to let the model continue.
- `"refusal"`: when streaming classifiers intervene to handle potential policy violations

In non-streaming mode this value is always non-null. In streaming mode, it is null in the `message_start` event and non-null otherwise.

One of the following:

END\_TURN("end\_turn")Óāö

MAX\_TOKENS("max\_tokens")Óāö

STOP\_SEQUENCE("stop\_sequence")Óāö

TOOL\_USE("tool\_use")Óāö

PAUSE\_TURN("pause\_turn")Óāö

COMPACTION("compaction")Óāö

REFUSAL("refusal")Óāö

MODEL\_CONTEXT\_WINDOW\_EXCEEDED("model\_context\_window\_exceeded")Óāö

ÓÄ™

Optional<String> stopSequenceÓāö

Which custom stop sequence was generated, if any.

This value will be a non-null string if one of your custom stop sequences was generated.

ÓÄ™

JsonValue; type "message"constantÓāö"message"constant

Object type.

For Messages, this is always `"message"`.

ÓÄ™

[BetaUsage](api/beta.md) usageÓāö

Billing and rate-limit usage.

Anthropic's API bills and rate-limits by token counts, as tokens represent the underlying cost to our systems.

Under the hood, the API transforms requests into a format suitable for the model. The model's output then goes through a parsing stage before becoming an API response. As a result, the token counts in `usage` will not match one-to-one with the exact visible content of an API request or response.

For example, `output_tokens` will be non-zero, even for an empty string response from Claude.

Total input tokens in a request is the summation of `input_tokens`, `cache_creation_input_tokens`, and `cache_read_input_tokens`.

ÓÄ™

Optional<[BetaCacheCreation](api/beta.md)> cacheCreationÓāö

Breakdown of cached tokens by TTL

long ephemeral1hInputTokensÓāö

The number of input tokens used to create the 1 hour cache entry.

long ephemeral5mInputTokensÓāö

The number of input tokens used to create the 5 minute cache entry.

Optional<Long> cacheCreationInputTokensÓāö

The number of input tokens used to create the cache entry.

Optional<Long> cacheReadInputTokensÓāö

The number of input tokens read from the cache.

Optional<String> inferenceGeoÓāö

The geographic region where inference was performed for this request.

long inputTokensÓāö

The number of input tokens which were used.

ÓÄ™

Optional<List<BetaIterationsUsageItems>> iterationsÓāö

Per-iteration token usage breakdown.

Each entry represents one sampling iteration, with its own input/output token counts and cache statistics. This allows you to:

- Determine which iterations exceeded long context thresholds (>=200k tokens)
- Calculate the true context window size from the last iteration
- Understand token accumulation across server-side tool use loops

One of the following:

ÓÄ™

class BetaMessageIterationUsage:Óāö

Token usage for a sampling iteration.

ÓÄ™

Breakdown of cached tokens by TTL

long ephemeral1hInputTokensÓāö

The number of input tokens used to create the 1 hour cache entry.

long ephemeral5mInputTokensÓāö

The number of input tokens used to create the 5 minute cache entry.

long cacheCreationInputTokensÓāö

The number of input tokens used to create the cache entry.

long cacheReadInputTokensÓāö

The number of input tokens read from the cache.

long inputTokensÓāö

The number of input tokens which were used.

entry in `usage.iterations`.

Optional<[BetaOutputTokensDetails](api/beta.md)> outputTokensDetailsÓāö

Breakdown of output tokens by category.

`output_tokens` remains the inclusive, authoritative total used for billing.
This object provides a read-only decomposition for observability ‚ÄĒ for example,
how many of the billed output tokens were spent on internal reasoning that may
have been summarized before being returned to you.

ÓÄ™

long thinkingTokensÓāö

Number of output tokens the model generated as internal reasoning, including
the thinking-block delimiter tokens.

Reflects the raw reasoning the model produced, not the (possibly shorter)
summarized thinking text returned in the response body. Computed by
re-tokenizing the raw reasoning text, so it may differ from the model's exact
generation count by a small number of tokens. Always ‚Č§ `output_tokens`;
`output_tokens - thinking_tokens` approximates the non-reasoning output.

minimum0

ÓÄ™

class BetaMessageBatchRequestCounts:Óāö

ÓÄ™

long canceledÓāö

Number of requests in the Message Batch that have been canceled.

This is zero until processing of the entire Message Batch has ended.

ÓÄ™

long erroredÓāö

Number of requests in the Message Batch that encountered an error.

This is zero until processing of the entire Message Batch has ended.

ÓÄ™

long expiredÓāö

Number of requests in the Message Batch that have expired.

This is zero until processing of the entire Message Batch has ended.

long processingÓāö

Number of requests in the Message Batch that are processing.

ÓÄ™

long succeededÓāö

Number of requests in the Message Batch that have completed successfully.

This is zero until processing of the entire Message Batch has ended.

ÓÄ™

class BetaMessageBatchResult: A class that can be one of several variants.union Óāö

Processing result for this request.

Contains a Message output if processing was successful, an error response if processing failed, or the reason why processing was not attempted, such as cancellation or expiration.

ÓÄ™

ÓÄ™

Type typeÓāö

Type of skill - either 'anthropic' (built-in) or 'custom' (user-defined)

One of the following:

ANTHROPIC("anthropic")Óāö

CUSTOM("custom")Óāö

String versionÓāö

Skill version or 'latest' for most recent version

ÓÄ™

List<[BetaContentBlock](api/beta.md)> contentÓāö

Content generated by the model.

This is an array of content blocks, each of which has a `type` that determines its shape.

Example:

```shiki
[{"type": "text", "text": "Hi, I'm Claude."}]
```

ÓĀĖ

If the request input `messages` ended with an `assistant` turn, then the response `content` will continue directly from that last turn. You can use this to constrain the model's output.

For example, if the input `messages` were:

```shiki
[
  {"role": "user", "content": "What's the Greek name for Sun? (A) Sol (B) Helios (C) Sun"},
  {"role": "assistant", "content": "The best answer is ("}
]
```

ÓĀĖ

Then the response `content` might be:

```shiki
[{"type": "text", "text": "B)"}]
```

ÓĀĖ

One of the following:

ÓÄ™

class BetaTextBlock:Óāö

ÓÄ™

Optional<List<[BetaTextCitation](api/beta.md)>> citationsÓāö

Citations supporting the text block.

The type of citation returned will depend on the type of document being cited. Citing a PDF results in `page_location`, plain text results in `char_location`, and content document results in `content_block_location`.

One of the following:

ÓÄ™

String citedTextÓāö

long documentIndexÓāö

Optional<String> documentTitleÓāö

long endCharIndexÓāö

Optional<String> fileIdÓāö

long startCharIndexÓāö

ÓÄ™

long documentIndexÓāö

Optional<String> documentTitleÓāö

class BetaCitationContentBlockLocation:Óāö

ÓÄ™

String citedTextÓāö

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

long documentIndexÓāö

Optional<String> documentTitleÓāö

ÓÄ™

long endBlockIndexÓāö

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

Optional<String> fileIdÓāö

long startBlockIndexÓāö

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

JsonValue; type "content\_block\_location"constantÓāö"content\_block\_location"constant

class BetaCompactionBlock:Óāö

A compaction block returned when autocompact is triggered.

When content is None, it indicates the compaction failed to produce a valid
summary (e.g., malformed output from the model). Clients may round-trip
compaction blocks with null content; the server treats them as no-ops.

Optional<String> contentÓāö

Summary of compacted content, or null if compaction failed

Optional<String> encryptedContentÓāö

Opaque metadata from prior compaction, to be round-tripped verbatim

JsonValue; type "compaction"constantÓāö"compaction"constant

ÓÄ™

class BetaFallbackBlock:Óāö

Marks the point in `content` where one model's output gives way to the next.

One block appears per hop where a preceding model actually ran this turn and
declined. A turn routed directly by the sticky decision has no such boundary
and carries no block ‚ÄĒ the signal for whether a fallback model served the
response is the presence of a `fallback_message` entry in
`usage.iterations`, not this block.

The block is treated like a server-tool content block for streaming: it
arrives via the standard `content_block_start` / `content_block_stop`
pair and carries no deltas.

ÓÄ™

[BetaFallbackInfo](api/beta.md) fromÓāö

The model whose output ends at this point ‚ÄĒ the model that declined at this hop. When the declining hop is the requested model, its `model` echoes the top-level `model` string the caller sent (alias or canonical); when the declining hop is a fallback model, its `model` is that model's canonical id.

ÓÄ™

Model modelÓāö

The model that will complete your prompt.

See [models](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/models-overview) for additional details and options.

One of the following:

CLAUDE\_FABLE\_5("claude-fable-5")Óāö

Next generation of intelligence for the hardest knowledge work and coding problems

CLAUDE\_MYTHOS\_5("claude-mythos-5")Óāö

Most capable model for cybersecurity and biology research

CLAUDE\_OPUS\_4\_8("claude-opus-4-8")Óāö

Frontier intelligence for long-running agents and coding

CLAUDE\_OPUS\_4\_7("claude-opus-4-7")Óāö

Frontier intelligence for long-running agents and coding

CLAUDE\_MYTHOS\_PREVIEW("claude-mythos-preview")Óāö

New class of intelligence, strongest in coding and cybersecurity

CLAUDE\_OPUS\_4\_6("claude-opus-4-6")Óāö

Frontier intelligence for long-running agents and coding

CLAUDE\_SONNET\_4\_6("claude-sonnet-4-6")Óāö

Best combination of speed and intelligence

CLAUDE\_HAIKU\_4\_5("claude-haiku-4-5")Óāö

Fastest model with near-frontier intelligence

CLAUDE\_HAIKU\_4\_5\_20251001("claude-haiku-4-5-20251001")Óāö

Fastest model with near-frontier intelligence

CLAUDE\_OPUS\_4\_5("claude-opus-4-5")Óāö

Premium model combining maximum intelligence with practical performance

CLAUDE\_OPUS\_4\_5\_20251101("claude-opus-4-5-20251101")Óāö

Premium model combining maximum intelligence with practical performance

CLAUDE\_SONNET\_4\_5("claude-sonnet-4-5")Óāö

High-performance model for agents and coding

CLAUDE\_SONNET\_4\_5\_20250929("claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929")Óāö

High-performance model for agents and coding

CLAUDE\_OPUS\_4\_1("claude-opus-4-1")Óāö

Exceptional model for specialized complex tasks

CLAUDE\_OPUS\_4\_1\_20250805("claude-opus-4-1-20250805")Óāö

Exceptional model for specialized complex tasks

CLAUDE\_OPUS\_4\_0("claude-opus-4-0")Óāö

Powerful model for complex tasks

CLAUDE\_OPUS\_4\_20250514("claude-opus-4-20250514")Óāö

JsonValue; role "assistant"constantÓāö"assistant"constant

Conversational role of the generated message.

This will always be `"assistant"`.

ÓÄ™

Optional<[BetaRefusalStopDetails](api/beta.md)> stopDetailsÓāö

Structured information about a refusal.

ÓÄ™

Optional<Category> categoryÓāö

The policy category that triggered the refusal.

`null` when the refusal doesn't map to a named category.

One of the following:

CYBER("cyber")Óāö

BIO("bio")Óāö

REASONING\_EXTRACTION("reasoning\_extraction")Óāö

ÓÄ™

Optional<String> explanationÓāö

Human-readable explanation of the refusal.

This text is not guaranteed to be stable. `null` when no explanation is available for the category.

ÓÄ™

Optional<String> fallbackCreditTokenÓāö

Opaque code that refunds the cache-miss cost when retrying this refused
request on the fallback model. Pass it as `fallback_credit_token` on the
retry request. Expires 5 minutes after the refusal.

The retry is sent either with the same request body (`system`, `messages`,
`tools`, and other render-shaping fields), or with the same body plus one
appended `assistant` message whose content is the partial text (with any
trailing whitespace stripped from the final text block) and paired
server-tool blocks from this refusal ‚ÄĒ which also authorizes that
appended turn as an assistant-prefill continuation on models that otherwise
disallow prefill. A token minted mid-server-tool-loop whose partial content
was continuable may only be redeemed the second way ‚ÄĒ if a same-body retry
is rejected with a 400 saying the token must be redeemed by continuing the
partial response, retry the second way instead. Either way: same workspace,
same platform; a mismatch is a 400. Resending a token for an already-warm
prefix is permitted but yields no additional credit.

`null` when the refused model isn't eligible for a fallback credit.

ÓÄ™

Optional<Boolean> fallbackHasPrefillClaimÓāö

Whether the accompanying `fallback_credit_token` may be redeemed with the
appended-assistant retry form. Only set when `fallback_credit_token` is
present.

`true`: retry by resending the same request body plus one appended
`assistant` message whose content is this response's `content` with any
trailing whitespace stripped from the final text block and unpaired
`tool_use` blocks omitted (the same appended-turn shape described on
`fallback_credit_token`), with the token attached. `false`: retry by
resending the original request body unchanged, with the token attached ‚ÄĒ
the appended-assistant form is not available for this refusal (no
continuable partial content, or the request uses `output_format` or a
`tool_choice` that forces tool use). One exception: when the request used
`output_format` or a forced `tool_choice` and the refusal arrived after
server tools (including MCP connector tools) had already executed, the
token may not be redeemable by either retry form; if the exact-body retry
is then rejected with a 400 saying the token must be redeemed by
continuing the partial response, discard the token and retry without it.

Advisory: if an appended-assistant retry is rejected with a 400 despite
`true`, fall back to resending the original request body with the token.

Optional<String> recommendedModelÓāö

The server's suggested retry target for this refusal. Populated when a fallback attempt could not be made (the fallback model's rate limit was exhausted, or it was overloaded); names the fallback model the caller can retry directly. Null otherwise.

JsonValue; type "refusal"constantÓāö"refusal"constant

ÓÄ™

Optional<[BetaStopReason](api/beta.md)> stopReasonÓāö

The reason that we stopped.

This may be one the following values:

- `"end_turn"`: the model reached a natural stopping point
- `"max_tokens"`: we exceeded the requested `max_tokens` or the model's maximum
- `"stop_sequence"`: one of your provided custom `stop_sequences` was generated
- `"tool_use"`: the model invoked one or more tools
- `"pause_turn"`: we paused a long-running turn. You may provide the response back as-is in a subsequent request to let the model continue.
- `"refusal"`: when streaming classifiers intervene to handle potential policy violations

In non-streaming mode this value is always non-null. In streaming mode, it is null in the `message_start` event and non-null otherwise.

One of the following:

END\_TURN("end\_turn")Óāö

MAX\_TOKENS("max\_tokens")Óāö

STOP\_SEQUENCE("stop\_sequence")Óāö

TOOL\_USE("tool\_use")Óāö

PAUSE\_TURN("pause\_turn")Óāö

COMPACTION("compaction")Óāö

REFUSAL("refusal")Óāö

MODEL\_CONTEXT\_WINDOW\_EXCEEDED("model\_context\_window\_exceeded")Óāö

ÓÄ™

Optional<String> stopSequenceÓāö

Which custom stop sequence was generated, if any.

This value will be a non-null string if one of your custom stop sequences was generated.

ÓÄ™

JsonValue; type "message"constantÓāö"message"constant

Object type.

For Messages, this is always `"message"`.

ÓÄ™

[BetaUsage](api/beta.md) usageÓāö

Billing and rate-limit usage.

Anthropic's API bills and rate-limits by token counts, as tokens represent the underlying cost to our systems.

Under the hood, the API transforms requests into a format suitable for the model. The model's output then goes through a parsing stage before becoming an API response. As a result, the token counts in `usage` will not match one-to-one with the exact visible content of an API request or response.

For example, `output_tokens` will be non-zero, even for an empty string response from Claude.

Total input tokens in a request is the summation of `input_tokens`, `cache_creation_input_tokens`, and `cache_read_input_tokens`.

ÓÄ™

Optional<[BetaCacheCreation](api/beta.md)> cacheCreationÓāö

Breakdown of cached tokens by TTL

long ephemeral1hInputTokensÓāö

The number of input tokens used to create the 1 hour cache entry.

long ephemeral5mInputTokensÓāö

The number of input tokens used to create the 5 minute cache entry.

Optional<Long> cacheCreationInputTokensÓāö

The number of input tokens used to create the cache entry.

Optional<Long> cacheReadInputTokensÓāö

The number of input tokens read from the cache.

Optional<String> inferenceGeoÓāö

The geographic region where inference was performed for this request.

long inputTokensÓāö

The number of input tokens which were used.

ÓÄ™

Optional<List<BetaIterationsUsageItems>> iterationsÓāö

Per-iteration token usage breakdown.

Each entry represents one sampling iteration, with its own input/output token counts and cache statistics. This allows you to:

- Determine which iterations exceeded long context thresholds (>=200k tokens)
- Calculate the true context window size from the last iteration
- Understand token accumulation across server-side tool use loops

One of the following:

ÓÄ™

class BetaMessageIterationUsage:Óāö

Token usage for a sampling iteration.

ÓÄ™

Optional<[BetaCacheCreation](api/beta.md)> cacheCreationÓāö

Breakdown of cached tokens by TTL

long ephemeral1hInputTokensÓāö

The number of input tokens used to create the 1 hour cache entry.

long ephemeral5mInputTokensÓāö

The number of input tokens used to create the 5 minute cache entry.

long cacheCreationInputTokensÓāö

The number of input tokens used to create the cache entry.

long cacheReadInputTokensÓāö

The number of input tokens read from the cache.

long inputTokensÓāö

The number of input tokens which were used.

ÓÄ™

class BetaFallbackMessageIterationUsage:Óāö

Token usage for the fallback-model attempt of a server-side fallback request.

Produced in place of a `message` entry for whichever hop served the
response. A declined hop produces the existing `message` entry. Whether
a fallback model served the response is signalled by the presence of this
entry in `usage.iterations`.

CLAUDE\_SONNET\_4\_0("claude-sonnet-4-0")Óāö

High-performance model with extended thinking

CLAUDE\_SONNET\_4\_20250514("claude-sonnet-4-20250514")Óāö

High-performance model with extended thinking

CLAUDE\_3\_HAIKU\_20240307("claude-3-haiku-20240307")Óāö

Fast and cost-effective model

long outputTokensÓāö

The number of output tokens which were used.

JsonValue; type "fallback\_message"constantÓāö"fallback\_message"constant

Usage for the fallback-model attempt that served the response

long outputTokensÓāö

The number of output tokens which were used.

ÓÄ™

Optional<[BetaOutputTokensDetails](api/beta.md)> outputTokensDetailsÓāö

Breakdown of output tokens by category.

`output_tokens` remains the inclusive, authoritative total used for billing.
This object provides a read-only decomposition for observability ‚ÄĒ for example,
how many of the billed output tokens were spent on internal reasoning that may
have been summarized before being returned to you.

ÓÄ™

long thinkingTokensÓāö

Number of output tokens the model generated as internal reasoning, including
the thinking-block delimiter tokens.

Reflects the raw reasoning the model produced, not the (possibly shorter)
summarized thinking text returned in the response body. Computed by
re-tokenizing the raw reasoning text, so it may differ from the model's exact
generation count by a small number of tokens. Always ‚Č§ `output_tokens`;
`output_tokens - thinking_tokens` approximates the non-reasoning output.

minimum0

ÓÄ™

Optional<[BetaServerToolUsage](api/beta.md)> serverToolUseÓāö

The number of server tool requests.

long webFetchRequestsÓāö

The number of web fetch tool requests.

long webSearchRequestsÓāö

The number of web search tool requests.

ÓÄ™

Optional<ServiceTier> serviceTierÓāö

If the request used the priority, standard, or batch tier.

One of the following:

STANDARD("standard")Óāö

PRIORITY("priority")Óāö

BATCH("batch")Óāö

ÓÄ™

Optional<Speed> speedÓāö

The inference speed mode used for this request.

One of the following:

STANDARD("standard")Óāö

FAST("fast")Óāö

JsonValue; type "succeeded"constantÓāö"succeeded"constant

ÓÄ™

ÓÄ™

Type typeÓāö

One of the following:

ANTHROPIC("anthropic")Óāö

CUSTOM("custom")Óāö

String versionÓāö

Skill version or 'latest' for most recent version

ÓÄ™

List<[BetaContentBlock](api/beta.md)> contentÓāö

Content generated by the model.

This is an array of content blocks, each of which has a `type` that determines its shape.

Example:

```shiki
[{"type": "text", "text": "Hi, I'm Claude."}]
```

ÓĀĖ

If the request input `messages` ended with an `assistant` turn, then the response `content` will continue directly from that last turn. You can use this to constrain the model's output.

For example, if the input `messages` were:

```shiki
[
  {"role": "user", "content": "What's the Greek name for Sun? (A) Sol (B) Helios (C) Sun"},
  {"role": "assistant", "content": "The best answer is ("}
]
```

ÓĀĖ

Then the response `content` might be:

```shiki
[{"type": "text", "text": "B)"}]
```

ÓĀĖ

One of the following:

ÓÄ™

class BetaTextBlock:Óāö

ÓÄ™

Optional<List<[BetaTextCitation](api/beta.md)>> citationsÓāö

Citations supporting the text block.

The type of citation returned will depend on the type of document being cited. Citing a PDF results in `page_location`, plain text results in `char_location`, and content document results in `content_block_location`.

One of the following:

ÓÄ™

class BetaCitationCharLocation:Óāö

String citedTextÓāö

long documentIndexÓāö

Optional<String> documentTitleÓāö

long endCharIndexÓāö

Optional<String> fileIdÓāö

long startCharIndexÓāö

JsonValue; type "char\_location"constantÓāö"char\_location"constant

ÓÄ™

class BetaCitationPageLocation:Óāö

String citedTextÓāö

long documentIndexÓāö

Optional<String> documentTitleÓāö

long endPageNumberÓāö

Optional<String> fileIdÓāö

long startPageNumberÓāö

JsonValue; type "page\_location"constantÓāö"page\_location"constant

ÓÄ™

class BetaCitationContentBlockLocation:Óāö

ÓÄ™

String citedTextÓāö

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

long documentIndexÓāö

Optional<String> documentTitleÓāö

ÓÄ™

long endBlockIndexÓāö

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

Optional<String> fileIdÓāö

long startBlockIndexÓāö

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

JsonValue; type "content\_block\_location"constantÓāö"content\_block\_location"constant

ÓÄ™

class BetaCitationsWebSearchResultLocation:Óāö

String citedTextÓāö

String encryptedIndexÓāö

Optional<String> titleÓāö

JsonValue; type "web\_search\_result\_location"constantÓāö"web\_search\_result\_location"constant

String urlÓāö

ÓÄ™

class BetaCitationSearchResultLocation:Óāö

ÓÄ™

String citedTextÓāö

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

ÓÄ™

long endBlockIndexÓāö

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

ÓÄ™

long searchResultIndexÓāö

0-based index of the cited search result among all `search_result` content blocks in the request, in the order they appear across messages and tool results.

Counted separately from `document_index`; server-side web search results are not included in this count.

minimum0

String sourceÓāö

long startBlockIndexÓāö

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

Optional<String> titleÓāö

JsonValue; type "search\_result\_location"constantÓāö"search\_result\_location"constant

JsonValue; type "text"constantÓāö"text"constant

class BetaCitationContentBlockLocation:Óāö

ÓÄ™

String citedTextÓāö

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

long documentIndexÓāö

Optional<String> documentTitleÓāö

ÓÄ™

long endBlockIndexÓāö

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

Optional<String> fileIdÓāö

long startBlockIndexÓāö

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

JsonValue; type "content\_block\_location"constantÓāö"content\_block\_location"constant

ÓÄ™

String citedTextÓāö

String encryptedIndexÓāö

Optional<String> titleÓāö

JsonValue; type "web\_search\_result\_location"constantÓāö"web\_search\_result\_location"constant

String urlÓāö

class BetaCitationSearchResultLocation:Óāö

ÓÄ™

String citedTextÓāö

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

ÓÄ™

long endBlockIndexÓāö

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

ÓÄ™

long searchResultIndexÓāö

0-based index of the cited search result among all `search_result` content blocks in the request, in the order they appear across messages and tool results.

Counted separately from `document_index`; server-side web search results are not included in this count.

minimum0

String sourceÓāö

long startBlockIndexÓāö

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

Optional<String> titleÓāö

String textÓāö

boolean isErrorÓāö

class BetaContainerUploadBlock:Óāö

Response model for a file uploaded to the container.

String fileIdÓāö

JsonValue; type "container\_upload"constantÓāö"container\_upload"constant

ÓÄ™

class BetaCompactionBlock:Óāö

A compaction block returned when autocompact is triggered.

When content is None, it indicates the compaction failed to produce a valid
summary (e.g., malformed output from the model). Clients may round-trip
compaction blocks with null content; the server treats them as no-ops.

Optional<String> contentÓāö

Summary of compacted content, or null if compaction failed

Optional<String> encryptedContentÓāö

Opaque metadata from prior compaction, to be round-tripped verbatim

JsonValue; type "compaction"constantÓāö"compaction"constant

ÓÄ™

class BetaFallbackBlock:Óāö

Marks the point in `content` where one model's output gives way to the next.

One block appears per hop where a preceding model actually ran this turn and
declined. A turn routed directly by the sticky decision has no such boundary
and carries no block ‚ÄĒ the signal for whether a fallback model served the
response is the presence of a `fallback_message` entry in
`usage.iterations`, not this block.

The block is treated like a server-tool content block for streaming: it
arrives via the standard `content_block_start` / `content_block_stop`
pair and carries no deltas.

ÓÄ™

[BetaFallbackInfo](api/beta.md) fromÓāö

The model whose output ends at this point ‚ÄĒ the model that declined at this hop. When the declining hop is the requested model, its `model` echoes the top-level `model` string the caller sent (alias or canonical); when the declining hop is a fallback model, its `model` is that model's canonical id.

ÓÄ™

Model modelÓāö

The model that will complete your prompt.

See [models](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/models-overview) for additional details and options.

JsonValue; role "assistant"constantÓāö"assistant"constant

Conversational role of the generated message.

This will always be `"assistant"`.

ÓÄ™

Optional<[BetaRefusalStopDetails](api/beta.md)> stopDetailsÓāö

Structured information about a refusal.

ÓÄ™

Optional<Category> categoryÓāö

The policy category that triggered the refusal.

`null` when the refusal doesn't map to a named category.

One of the following:

CYBER("cyber")Óāö

BIO("bio")Óāö

REASONING\_EXTRACTION("reasoning\_extraction")Óāö

ÓÄ™

Optional<String> explanationÓāö

Human-readable explanation of the refusal.

This text is not guaranteed to be stable. `null` when no explanation is available for the category.

ÓÄ™

Optional<String> fallbackCreditTokenÓāö

Opaque code that refunds the cache-miss cost when retrying this refused
request on the fallback model. Pass it as `fallback_credit_token` on the
retry request. Expires 5 minutes after the refusal.

The retry is sent either with the same request body (`system`, `messages`,
`tools`, and other render-shaping fields), or with the same body plus one
appended `assistant` message whose content is the partial text (with any
trailing whitespace stripped from the final text block) and paired
server-tool blocks from this refusal ‚ÄĒ which also authorizes that
appended turn as an assistant-prefill continuation on models that otherwise
disallow prefill. A token minted mid-server-tool-loop whose partial content
was continuable may only be redeemed the second way ‚ÄĒ if a same-body retry
is rejected with a 400 saying the token must be redeemed by continuing the
partial response, retry the second way instead. Either way: same workspace,
same platform; a mismatch is a 400. Resending a token for an already-warm
prefix is permitted but yields no additional credit.

`null` when the refused model isn't eligible for a fallback credit.

ÓÄ™

Optional<Boolean> fallbackHasPrefillClaimÓāö

Whether the accompanying `fallback_credit_token` may be redeemed with the
appended-assistant retry form. Only set when `fallback_credit_token` is
present.

`true`: retry by resending the same request body plus one appended
`assistant` message whose content is this response's `content` with any
trailing whitespace stripped from the final text block and unpaired
`tool_use` blocks omitted (the same appended-turn shape described on
`fallback_credit_token`), with the token attached. `false`: retry by
resending the original request body unchanged, with the token attached ‚ÄĒ
the appended-assistant form is not available for this refusal (no
continuable partial content, or the request uses `output_format` or a
`tool_choice` that forces tool use). One exception: when the request used
`output_format` or a forced `tool_choice` and the refusal arrived after
server tools (including MCP connector tools) had already executed, the
token may not be redeemable by either retry form; if the exact-body retry
is then rejected with a 400 saying the token must be redeemed by
continuing the partial response, discard the token and retry without it.

Advisory: if an appended-assistant retry is rejected with a 400 despite
`true`, fall back to resending the original request body with the token.

Optional<String> recommendedModelÓāö

The server's suggested retry target for this refusal. Populated when a fallback attempt could not be made (the fallback model's rate limit was exhausted, or it was overloaded); names the fallback model the caller can retry directly. Null otherwise.

JsonValue; type "refusal"constantÓāö"refusal"constant

ÓÄ™

Optional<[BetaStopReason](api/beta.md)> stopReasonÓāö

The reason that we stopped.

This may be one the following values:

- `"end_turn"`: the model reached a natural stopping point
- `"max_tokens"`: we exceeded the requested `max_tokens` or the model's maximum
- `"stop_sequence"`: one of your provided custom `stop_sequences` was generated
- `"tool_use"`: the model invoked one or more tools
- `"pause_turn"`: we paused a long-running turn. You may provide the response back as-is in a subsequent request to let the model continue.
- `"refusal"`: when streaming classifiers intervene to handle potential policy violations

In non-streaming mode this value is always non-null. In streaming mode, it is null in the `message_start` event and non-null otherwise.

One of the following:

END\_TURN("end\_turn")Óāö

MAX\_TOKENS("max\_tokens")Óāö

STOP\_SEQUENCE("stop\_sequence")Óāö

TOOL\_USE("tool\_use")Óāö

PAUSE\_TURN("pause\_turn")Óāö

COMPACTION("compaction")Óāö

REFUSAL("refusal")Óāö

MODEL\_CONTEXT\_WINDOW\_EXCEEDED("model\_context\_window\_exceeded")Óāö

ÓÄ™

Optional<String> stopSequenceÓāö

Which custom stop sequence was generated, if any.

This value will be a non-null string if one of your custom stop sequences was generated.

ÓÄ™

JsonValue; type "message"constantÓāö"message"constant

Object type.

For Messages, this is always `"message"`.

ÓÄ™

[BetaUsage](api/beta.md) usageÓāö

Billing and rate-limit usage.

Anthropic's API bills and rate-limits by token counts, as tokens represent the underlying cost to our systems.

Under the hood, the API transforms requests into a format suitable for the model. The model's output then goes through a parsing stage before becoming an API response. As a result, the token counts in `usage` will not match one-to-one with the exact visible content of an API request or response.

For example, `output_tokens` will be non-zero, even for an empty string response from Claude.

Total input tokens in a request is the summation of `input_tokens`, `cache_creation_input_tokens`, and `cache_read_input_tokens`.

ÓÄ™

Optional<[BetaCacheCreation](api/beta.md)> cacheCreationÓāö

Breakdown of cached tokens by TTL

long ephemeral1hInputTokensÓāö

The number of input tokens used to create the 1 hour cache entry.

long ephemeral5mInputTokensÓāö

The number of input tokens used to create the 5 minute cache entry.

Optional<Long> cacheCreationInputTokensÓāö

The number of input tokens used to create the cache entry.

Optional<Long> cacheReadInputTokensÓāö

The number of input tokens read from the cache.

Optional<String> inferenceGeoÓāö

The geographic region where inference was performed for this request.

long inputTokensÓāö

The number of input tokens which were used.

ÓÄ™

Optional<List<BetaIterationsUsageItems>> iterationsÓāö

Per-iteration token usage breakdown.

Each entry represents one sampling iteration, with its own input/output token counts and cache statistics. This allows you to:

- Determine which iterations exceeded long context thresholds (>=200k tokens)
- Calculate the true context window size from the last iteration
- Understand token accumulation across server-side tool use loops

One of the following:

ÓÄ™

class BetaMessageIterationUsage:Óāö

Token usage for a sampling iteration.

ÓÄ™

Optional<[BetaCacheCreation](api/beta.md)> cacheCreationÓāö

Breakdown of cached tokens by TTL

long ephemeral1hInputTokensÓāö

The number of input tokens used to create the 1 hour cache entry.

long ephemeral5mInputTokensÓāö

The number of input tokens used to create the 5 minute cache entry.

long cacheCreationInputTokensÓāö

The number of input tokens used to create the cache entry.

long cacheReadInputTokensÓāö

The number of input tokens read from the cache.

long inputTokensÓāö

The number of input tokens which were used.

ÓÄ™

See [models](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/models-overview) for additional details and options.

class BetaFallbackMessageIterationUsage:Óāö

Token usage for the fallback-model attempt of a server-side fallback request.

Produced in place of a `message` entry for whichever hop served the
response. A declined hop produces the existing `message` entry. Whether
a fallback model served the response is signalled by the presence of this
entry in `usage.iterations`.

ÓÄ™

Optional<[BetaOutputTokensDetails](api/beta.md)> outputTokensDetailsÓāö

Breakdown of output tokens by category.

`output_tokens` remains the inclusive, authoritative total used for billing.
This object provides a read-only decomposition for observability ‚ÄĒ for example,
how many of the billed output tokens were spent on internal reasoning that may
have been summarized before being returned to you.

ÓÄ™

long thinkingTokensÓāö

Number of output tokens the model generated as internal reasoning, including
the thinking-block delimiter tokens.

Reflects the raw reasoning the model produced, not the (possibly shorter)
summarized thinking text returned in the response body. Computed by
re-tokenizing the raw reasoning text, so it may differ from the model's exact
generation count by a small number of tokens. Always ‚Č§ `output_tokens`;
`output_tokens - thinking_tokens` approximates the non-reasoning output.

minimum0

ÓÄ™

long webFetchRequestsÓāö

The number of web fetch tool requests.

long webSearchRequestsÓāö

The number of web search tool requests.

ÓÄ™

#### BetaAgents

##### [Create Agent](api/beta/agents/create.md)

[BetaManagedAgentsAgent](api/beta.md) beta().agents().create(AgentCreateParamsparams, RequestOptionsrequestOptions = RequestOptions.none())

POST/v1/agents

##### [List Agents](api/beta/agents/list.md)

AgentListPage beta().agents().list(AgentListParamsparams = AgentListParams.none(), RequestOptionsrequestOptions = RequestOptions.none())

GET/v1/agents

##### [Get Agent](api/beta/agents/retrieve.md)

[BetaManagedAgentsAgent](api/beta.md) beta().agents().retrieve(AgentRetrieveParamsparams = AgentRetrieveParams.none(), RequestOptionsrequestOptions = RequestOptions.none())

GET/v1/agents/{agent\_id}

##### [Update Agent](api/beta/agents/update.md)

[BetaManagedAgentsAgent](api/beta.md) beta().agents().update(AgentUpdateParamsparams, RequestOptionsrequestOptions = RequestOptions.none())

POST/v1/agents/{agent\_id}

##### [Archive Agent](api/beta/agents/archive.md)

[BetaManagedAgentsAgent](api/beta.md) beta().agents().archive(AgentArchiveParamsparams = AgentArchiveParams.none(), RequestOptionsrequestOptions = RequestOptions.none())

POST/v1/agents/{agent\_id}/archive

class BetaManagedAgentsAgentToolset20260401BashInput:Óāö

Input payload for the `bash` tool of the
`agent_toolset_20260401` toolset. All fields are optional;
a normal invocation supplies `command`, while `restart=true`
(with no `command`) reboots the runner-side bash session.

Optional<String> commandÓāö

Shell command to execute. Omit only when `restart` is true.

Optional<Boolean> restartÓāö

When true, restart the persistent bash session instead of
running a command. Subsequent calls without `restart` will
run against the fresh session.

Optional<Long> timeoutMsÓāö

Per-call timeout in milliseconds. Defaults to the
runner-wide tool timeout when omitted or zero.

ÓÄ™

class BetaManagedAgentsAgentToolset20260401EditInput:Óāö

Input payload for the `edit` tool. Performs a string
replacement in the named file; by default `old_string` must
occur exactly once.

String filePathÓāö

Path of the file to edit.

String newStringÓāö

Replacement text.

String oldStringÓāö

Substring to find and replace.

Optional<Boolean> replaceAllÓāö

When true, replace every occurrence of `old_string`
instead of requiring a unique match.

ÓÄ™

class BetaManagedAgentsAgentToolset20260401GlobInput:Óāö

Input payload for the `glob` tool. Returns paths matching a
doublestar glob pattern, newest first.

String patternÓāö

Doublestar glob pattern (e.g. `**/*.go`). Absolute patterns
are only permitted when the runner is configured to allow
them.

Optional<String> pathÓāö

Optional directory root to search under. Defaults to the
runner's working directory.

ÓÄ™

class BetaManagedAgentsAgentToolset20260401GrepInput:Óāö

Input payload for the `grep` tool. Searches file contents for
a regular expression, returning matching lines.

String patternÓāö

Regular expression to search for.

Optional<String> pathÓāö

Optional directory root to search under. Defaults to the
runner's working directory.

ÓÄ™

class BetaManagedAgentsAgentToolset20260401ReadInput:Óāö

Input payload for the `read` tool. Reads file contents
relative to the runner's working directory (or absolute when
the runner permits).

Path of the file to read.

Optional<List<Long>> viewRangeÓāö

range. When omitted the entire file is returned.
`end_line` of 0 or negative means "to end of file".

class BetaManagedAgentsAgentToolset20260401WriteInput:Óāö

Input payload for the `write` tool. Writes (overwriting) the

class BetaManagedAgentsCustomToolParams:Óāö

A custom tool that is executed by the API client rather than the agent. When the agent calls this tool, an `agent.custom_tool_use` event is emitted and the session goes idle, waiting for the client to provide the result via a `user.custom_tool_result` event.

String descriptionÓāö

Description of what the tool does, shown to the agent to help it decide when to use the tool. 1-1024 characters.

enum BetaManagedAgentsModel:Óāö

The model that will power your agent.

See [models](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/models-overview) for additional details and options.

CLAUDE\_FABLE\_5("claude-fable-5")Óāö

Next generation of intelligence for the hardest knowledge work and coding problems

CLAUDE\_OPUS\_4\_8("claude-opus-4-8")Óāö

Frontier intelligence for long-running agents and coding

CLAUDE\_OPUS\_4\_7("claude-opus-4-7")Óāö

Frontier intelligence for long-running agents and coding

CLAUDE\_OPUS\_4\_6("claude-opus-4-6")Óāö

Most intelligent model for building agents and coding

CLAUDE\_SONNET\_4\_6("claude-sonnet-4-6")Óāö

Best combination of speed and intelligence

CLAUDE\_HAIKU\_4\_5("claude-haiku-4-5")Óāö

Fastest model with near-frontier intelligence

CLAUDE\_HAIKU\_4\_5\_20251001("claude-haiku-4-5-20251001")Óāö

Fastest model with near-frontier intelligence

CLAUDE\_OPUS\_4\_5("claude-opus-4-5")Óāö

Premium model combining maximum intelligence with practical performance

CLAUDE\_OPUS\_4\_5\_20251101("claude-opus-4-5-20251101")Óāö

Premium model combining maximum intelligence with practical performance

CLAUDE\_SONNET\_4\_5("claude-sonnet-4-5")Óāö

High-performance model for agents and coding

CLAUDE\_SONNET\_4\_5\_20250929("claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929")Óāö

High-performance model for agents and coding

class BetaManagedAgentsMultiagentCoordinator:Óāö

Resolved coordinator topology with a concrete agent roster.

ÓÄ™

List<[BetaManagedAgentsAgentReference](api/beta.md)> agentsÓāö

Agents the coordinator may spawn as session threads, each resolved to a specific version.

String idÓāö

Type typeÓāö

long versionÓāö

Type typeÓāö

ÓÄ™

class BetaManagedAgentsMultiagentCoordinatorParams:Óāö

A coordinator topology: the session's primary thread orchestrates work by spawning session threads, each running an agent drawn from the `agents` roster.

ÓÄ™

List<[BetaManagedAgentsMultiagentRosterEntryParams](api/beta.md)> agentsÓāö

Agents the coordinator may spawn as session threads. 1‚Äď20 entries. Each entry is an agent ID string, a versioned `{"type":"agent","id","version"}` reference, or `{"type":"self"}` to allow recursive self-invocation. Entries must reference distinct agents (after resolving `self` and string forms); at most one `self`. Referenced agents must exist, must not be archived, and must not themselves have `multiagent` set (depth limit 1).

One of the following:

StringÓāö

ÓÄ™

class BetaManagedAgentsAgentParams:Óāö

Specification for an Agent. Provide a specific `version` or use the short-form `agent="agent_id"` for the most recent version

String idÓāö

The `agent` ID.

Type typeÓāö

Optional<Long> versionÓāö

The specific `agent` version to use. Omit to use the latest version. Must be at least 1 if specified.

ÓÄ™

class BetaManagedAgentsMultiagentSelfParams:Óāö

Sentinel roster entry meaning "the agent that owns this configuration". Resolved server-side to a concrete agent reference.

Type typeÓāö

Type typeÓāö

ÓÄ™

class BetaManagedAgentsMultiagentSelfParams:Óāö

Sentinel roster entry meaning "the agent that owns this configuration". Resolved server-side to a concrete agent reference.

Type typeÓāö

ÓÄ™

#### BetaAgentsVersions

##### [List Agent Versions](api/beta/agents/versions/list.md)

VersionListPage beta().agents().versions().list(VersionListParamsparams = VersionListParams.none(), RequestOptionsrequestOptions = RequestOptions.none())

GET/v1/agents/{agent\_id}/versions

#### BetaEnvironments

##### [Create Environment](api/beta/environments/create.md)

[BetaEnvironment](api/beta.md) beta().environments().create(EnvironmentCreateParamsparams, RequestOptionsrequestOptions = RequestOptions.none())

POST/v1/environments

##### [List Environments](api/beta/environments/list.md)

EnvironmentListPage beta().environments().list(EnvironmentListParamsparams = EnvironmentListParams.none(), RequestOptionsrequestOptions = RequestOptions.none())

GET/v1/environments

##### [Get Environment](api/beta/environments/retrieve.md)

[BetaEnvironment](api/beta.md) beta().environments().retrieve(EnvironmentRetrieveParamsparams = EnvironmentRetrieveParams.none(), RequestOptionsrequestOptions = RequestOptions.none())

GET/v1/environments/{environment\_id}

##### [Update Environment](api/beta/environments/update.md)

[BetaEnvironment](api/beta.md) beta().environments().update(EnvironmentUpdateParamsparams = EnvironmentUpdateParams.none(), RequestOptionsrequestOptions = RequestOptions.none())

POST/v1/environments/{environment\_id}

##### [Delete Environment](api/beta/environments/delete.md)

[BetaEnvironmentDeleteResponse](api/beta.md) beta().environments().delete(EnvironmentDeleteParamsparams = EnvironmentDeleteParams.none(), RequestOptionsrequestOptions = RequestOptions.none())

DELETE/v1/environments/{environment\_id}

##### [Archive Environment](api/beta/environments/archive.md)

[BetaEnvironment](api/beta.md) beta().environments().archive(EnvironmentArchiveParamsparams = EnvironmentArchiveParams.none(), RequestOptionsrequestOptions = RequestOptions.none())

POST/v1/environments/{environment\_id}/archive

class BetaSelfHostedWork:Óāö

Work resource representing a unit of work in a self-hosted environment.

Work items are queued when sessions are created or when long-dormant sessions
receive new messages. The environment worker polls for work to execute in a
self-hosted sandbox.

String idÓāö

Work identifier (e.g., 'work\_...')

Optional<String> acknowledgedAtÓāö

RFC 3339 timestamp when the work item was acknowledged and assigned to a self-hosted sandbox

String createdAtÓāö

RFC 3339 timestamp when work was created

ÓÄ™

[BetaSessionWorkData](api/beta.md) dataÓāö

The actual work to be performed

String idÓāö

Session identifier (e.g., 'session\_...')

JsonValue; type "session"constantÓāö"session"constant

Type of work data

String environmentIdÓāö

Environment identifier this work belongs to (e.g., `env_...`)

Optional<String> latestHeartbeatAtÓāö

RFC 3339 timestamp of the most recent heartbeat

Metadata metadataÓāö

User-provided metadata key-value pairs associated with this work item

Optional<String> startedAtÓāö

RFC 3339 timestamp when work execution started

ÓÄ™

State stateÓāö

Current state of the work item

One of the following:

QUEUED("queued")Óāö

STARTING("starting")Óāö

ACTIVE("active")Óāö

STOPPING("stopping")Óāö

STOPPED("stopped")Óāö

Optional<String> stopRequestedAtÓāö

RFC 3339 timestamp when stop was requested

Optional<String> stoppedAtÓāö

RFC 3339 timestamp when work execution stopped

JsonValue; type "work"constantÓāö"work"constant

The type of object (always 'work')

ÓÄ™

String lastHeartbeatÓāö

RFC 3339 timestamp of the actual heartbeat from DB

boolean leaseExtendedÓāö

Whether the heartbeat succeeded in extending the lease

ÓÄ™

State stateÓāö

Current state of the work item (active/stopping/stopped)

One of the following:

QUEUED("queued")Óāö

STARTING("starting")Óāö

ACTIVE("active")Óāö

STOPPING("stopping")Óāö

STOPPED("stopped")Óāö

long ttlSecondsÓāö

class BetaSelfHostedWorkQueueStats:Óāö

Statistics about the work queue for an environment.

Uses Redis Stream consumer group metrics for O(1) queries.

long depthÓāö

Number of work items waiting to be picked up (lag from consumer group)

Optional<String> oldestQueuedAtÓāö

RFC 3339 timestamp of oldest item in the work stream (includes both queued and pending items), null if stream empty

long pendingÓāö

Number of work items being processed (polled but not acknowledged)

JsonValue; type "work\_queue\_stats"constantÓāö"work\_queue\_stats"constant

The type of object

Optional<Long> workersPollingÓāö

Number of workers that have polled for work in the last 30 seconds. Requires worker\_id to be sent with poll requests.

ÓÄ™

Request to stop a work item.

Optional<Boolean> forceÓāö

If true, immediately stop work without graceful shutdown

ÓÄ™

Metadata patch. Set a key to a string to upsert it, or to null to delete it. Omit the field to preserve existing metadata.

class BetaManagedAgentsMultiagent:Óāö

long versionÓāö

class BetaManagedAgentsMultiagentParams:Óāö

A coordinator topology: the session's primary thread orchestrates work by spawning session threads, each running an agent drawn from the `agents` roster.

ÓÄ™

List<[BetaManagedAgentsMultiagentRosterEntryParams](api/beta.md)> agentsÓāö

Agents the coordinator may spawn as session threads. 1‚Äď20 entries. Each entry is an agent ID string, a versioned `{"type":"agent","id","version"}` reference, or `{"type":"self"}` to allow recursive self-invocation. Entries must reference distinct agents (after resolving `self` and string forms); at most one `self`. Referenced agents must exist, must not be archived, and must not themselves have `multiagent` set (depth limit 1).

One of the following:

StringÓāö

ÓÄ™

The specific `agent` version to use. Omit to use the latest version. Must be at least 1 if specified.

class BetaManagedAgentsOutcomeEvaluationResource:Óāö

Evaluation state for a single outcome defined via a define\_outcome event.

Optional<LocalDateTime> completedAtÓāö

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

String descriptionÓāö

What the agent should produce.

Optional<String> explanationÓāö

Grader's verdict text from the most recent evaluation. For satisfied, explains why criteria are met; for needs\_revision (intermediate), what's missing; for failed, why unrecoverable.

long iterationÓāö

0-indexed revision cycle the outcome is currently on.

String outcomeIdÓāö

Server-generated outc\_ ID for this outcome.

String resultÓāö

Current evaluation state. `pending` before the agent begins work; `running` while producing or revising; `evaluating` while the grader scores; `satisfied`/`max_iterations_reached`/`failed`/`interrupted` are terminal.

Type typeÓāö

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsModel idÓāö

The model that will power your agent.

One of the following:

CLAUDE\_FABLE\_5("claude-fable-5")Óāö

Next generation of intelligence for the hardest knowledge work and coding problems

CLAUDE\_OPUS\_4\_8("claude-opus-4-8")Óāö

Frontier intelligence for long-running agents and coding

CLAUDE\_OPUS\_4\_7("claude-opus-4-7")Óāö

Frontier intelligence for long-running agents and coding

CLAUDE\_OPUS\_4\_6("claude-opus-4-6")Óāö

Most intelligent model for building agents and coding

CLAUDE\_SONNET\_4\_6("claude-sonnet-4-6")Óāö

Best combination of speed and intelligence

CLAUDE\_HAIKU\_4\_5("claude-haiku-4-5")Óāö

Fastest model with near-frontier intelligence

CLAUDE\_HAIKU\_4\_5\_20251001("claude-haiku-4-5-20251001")Óāö

Fastest model with near-frontier intelligence

CLAUDE\_OPUS\_4\_5("claude-opus-4-5")Óāö

Premium model combining maximum intelligence with practical performance

CLAUDE\_OPUS\_4\_5\_20251101("claude-opus-4-5-20251101")Óāö

Premium model combining maximum intelligence with practical performance

CLAUDE\_SONNET\_4\_5("claude-sonnet-4-5")Óāö

High-performance model for agents and coding

CLAUDE\_SONNET\_4\_5\_20250929("claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929")Óāö

High-performance model for agents and coding

ÓÄ™

Optional<Speed> speedÓāö

Inference speed mode. `fast` provides significantly faster output token generation at premium pricing. Not all models support `fast`; invalid combinations are rejected at create time.

STANDARD("standard")Óāö

FAST("fast")Óāö

long versionÓāö

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

ÓÄ™

List<[BetaManagedAgentsOutcomeEvaluationResource](api/beta.md)> outcomeEvaluationsÓāö

Per-outcome evaluation state. One entry per define\_outcome event sent to the session.

Optional<LocalDateTime> completedAtÓāö

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

String descriptionÓāö

What the agent should produce.

Optional<String> explanationÓāö

Grader's verdict text from the most recent evaluation. For satisfied, explains why criteria are met; for needs\_revision (intermediate), what's missing; for failed, why unrecoverable.

long iterationÓāö

0-indexed revision cycle the outcome is currently on.

String outcomeIdÓāö

Server-generated outc\_ ID for this outcome.

String resultÓāö

Current evaluation state. `pending` before the agent begins work; `running` while producing or revising; `evaluating` while the grader scores; `satisfied`/`max_iterations_reached`/`failed`/`interrupted` are terminal.

Type typeÓāö

ÓÄ™

class BetaManagedAgentsMemoryStoreResource:Óāö

A memory store attached to an agent session.

String memoryStoreIdÓāö

The memory store ID (memstore\_...). Must belong to the caller's organization and workspace.

Type typeÓāö

ÓÄ™

Optional<Access> accessÓāö

Access mode for an attached memory store.

One of the following:

READ\_WRITE("read\_write")Óāö

READ\_ONLY("read\_only")Óāö

Optional<String> descriptionÓāö

Description of the memory store, snapshotted at attach time. Rendered into the agent's system prompt. Empty string when the store has no description.

Optional<String> instructionsÓāö

Per-attachment guidance for the agent on how to use this store. Rendered into the memory section of the system prompt. Max 4096 chars.

Optional<String> mountPathÓāö

Filesystem path where the store is mounted in the session container, e.g. /mnt/memory/user-preferences. Derived from the store's name. Output-only.

Optional<String> nameÓāö

Display name of the memory store, snapshotted at attach time. Later edits to the store's name do not propagate to this resource.

ÓÄ™

[BetaManagedAgentsSessionStats](api/beta.md) statsÓāö

Timing statistics for a session.

Optional<Double> activeSecondsÓāö

Cumulative time in seconds the session spent in running status. Excludes idle time.

Optional<Double> durationSecondsÓāö

Elapsed time since session creation in seconds. For terminated sessions, frozen at the final update.

ÓÄ™

Status statusÓāö

SessionStatus enum

One of the following:

RESCHEDULING("rescheduling")Óāö

RUNNING("running")Óāö

IDLE("idle")Óāö

TERMINATED("terminated")Óāö

Optional<String> titleÓāö

Type typeÓāö

LocalDateTime updatedAtÓāö

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

ÓÄ™

[BetaManagedAgentsSessionUsage](api/beta.md) usageÓāö

Cumulative token usage for a session across all turns.

ÓÄ™

Optional<[BetaManagedAgentsCacheCreationUsage](api/beta.md)> cacheCreationÓāö

Prompt-cache creation token usage broken down by cache lifetime.

Optional<Long> ephemeral1hInputTokensÓāö

Tokens used to create 1-hour ephemeral cache entries.

Optional<Long> ephemeral5mInputTokensÓāö

Tokens used to create 5-minute ephemeral cache entries.

Optional<Long> cacheReadInputTokensÓāö

Total tokens read from prompt cache.

Optional<Long> inputTokensÓāö

Total input tokens consumed across all turns.

Optional<Long> outputTokensÓāö

Total output tokens generated across all turns.

List<String> vaultIdsÓāö

Vault IDs attached to the session at creation. Empty when no vaults were supplied.

Optional<String> deploymentIdÓāö

Deployment ID when the session was created from a deployment reference. Null otherwise.

ÓÄ™

CLAUDE\_FABLE\_5("claude-fable-5")Óāö

CLAUDE\_OPUS\_4\_8("claude-opus-4-8")Óāö

class BetaManagedAgentsSessionAgentUpdate:Óāö

Mid-session agent configuration update. Only `tools` and `mcp_servers` are updatable. Full replacement: the provided array becomes the new value. To preserve existing entries, GET the session, modify the array, and POST it back.

ÓÄ™

Optional<List<[BetaManagedAgentsUrlMcpServerParams](api/beta.md)>> mcpServersÓāö

Replacement MCP server list. Full replacement: the provided array becomes the new value. Send an empty array to clear; omit to preserve.

String nameÓāö

Unique name for this server, referenced by mcp\_toolset configurations. 1-255 characters.

Type typeÓāö

String urlÓāö

Endpoint URL for the MCP server.

ÓÄ™

Optional<List<Tool>> toolsÓāö

Replacement tool list. Full replacement: the provided array becomes the new value. Send an empty array to clear; omit to preserve.

One of the following:

ÓÄ™

class BetaManagedAgentsAgentToolset20260401Params:Óāö

Configuration for built-in agent tools. Use this to enable or disable groups of tools available to the agent.

Type typeÓāö

ÓÄ™

Optional<List<[BetaManagedAgentsAgentToolConfigParams](api/beta.md)>> configsÓāö

Per-tool configuration overrides.

ÓÄ™

Name nameÓāö

Built-in agent tool identifier.

One of the following:

BASH("bash")Óāö

EDIT("edit")Óāö

READ("read")Óāö

WRITE("write")Óāö

GLOB("glob")Óāö

GREP("grep")Óāö

WEB\_FETCH("web\_fetch")Óāö

WEB\_SEARCH("web\_search")Óāö

Optional<Boolean> enabledÓāö

Whether this tool is enabled and available to Claude. Overrides the default\_config setting.

ÓÄ™

Optional<PermissionPolicy> permissionPolicyÓāö

Permission policy for tool execution.

One of the following:

ÓÄ™

class BetaManagedAgentsAlwaysAllowPolicy:Óāö

Tool calls are automatically approved without user confirmation.

Type typeÓāö

ÓÄ™

class BetaManagedAgentsAlwaysAskPolicy:Óāö

Tool calls require user confirmation before execution.

Type typeÓāö

ÓÄ™

Optional<[BetaManagedAgentsAgentToolsetDefaultConfigParams](api/beta.md)> defaultConfigÓāö

Default configuration for all tools in a toolset.

Optional<Boolean> enabledÓāö

Whether tools are enabled and available to Claude by default. Defaults to true if not specified.

ÓÄ™

Optional<PermissionPolicy> permissionPolicyÓāö

Permission policy for tool execution.

One of the following:

ÓÄ™

class BetaManagedAgentsAlwaysAllowPolicy:Óāö

Tool calls are automatically approved without user confirmation.

Type typeÓāö

ÓÄ™

class BetaManagedAgentsAlwaysAskPolicy:Óāö

Tool calls require user confirmation before execution.

Type typeÓāö

ÓÄ™

class BetaManagedAgentsMcpToolsetParams:Óāö

Configuration for tools from an MCP server defined in `mcp_servers`.

String mcpServerNameÓāö

Name of the MCP server. Must match a server name from the mcp\_servers array. 1-255 characters.

Type typeÓāö

ÓÄ™

Optional<List<[BetaManagedAgentsMcpToolConfigParams](api/beta.md)>> configsÓāö

Per-tool configuration overrides.

String nameÓāö

Name of the MCP tool to configure. 1-128 characters.

Optional<Boolean> enabledÓāö

Whether this tool is enabled. Overrides the `default_config` setting.

ÓÄ™

Optional<PermissionPolicy> permissionPolicyÓāö

Permission policy for tool execution.

One of the following:

ÓÄ™

class BetaManagedAgentsAlwaysAllowPolicy:Óāö

Tool calls are automatically approved without user confirmation.

Type typeÓāö

ÓÄ™

class BetaManagedAgentsAlwaysAskPolicy:Óāö

Tool calls require user confirmation before execution.

Type typeÓāö

ÓÄ™

Optional<[BetaManagedAgentsMcpToolsetDefaultConfigParams](api/beta.md)> defaultConfigÓāö

Default configuration for all tools from an MCP server.

Optional<Boolean> enabledÓāö

Whether tools are enabled by default. Defaults to true if not specified.

ÓÄ™

Optional<PermissionPolicy> permissionPolicyÓāö

Permission policy for tool execution.

One of the following:

ÓÄ™

class BetaManagedAgentsAlwaysAllowPolicy:Óāö

Tool calls are automatically approved without user confirmation.

Type typeÓāö

ÓÄ™

class BetaManagedAgentsAlwaysAskPolicy:Óāö

Tool calls require user confirmation before execution.

Type typeÓāö

ÓÄ™

class BetaManagedAgentsCustomToolParams:Óāö

A custom tool that is executed by the API client rather than the agent. When the agent calls this tool, an `agent.custom_tool_use` event is emitted and the session goes idle, waiting for the client to provide the result via a `user.custom_tool_result` event.

String descriptionÓāö

Description of what the tool does, shown to the agent to help it decide when to use the tool. 1-1024 characters.

ÓÄ™

[BetaManagedAgentsCustomToolInputSchema](api/beta.md) inputSchemaÓāö

JSON Schema for custom tool input parameters.

JsonValue; type "object"constantÓāö"object"constant

Optional<Properties> propertiesÓāö

Optional<List<String>> requiredÓāö

String nameÓāö

Unique name for the tool. 1-128 characters; letters, digits, underscores, and hyphens.

Type typeÓāö

ÓÄ™

class BetaManagedAgentsSessionMultiagentCoordinator:Óāö

Resolved coordinator topology with full agent definitions for each roster member.

ÓÄ™

List<[BetaManagedAgentsSessionThreadAgent](api/beta.md)> agentsÓāö

Full `agent` definitions the coordinator may spawn as session threads.

String idÓāö

Optional<String> descriptionÓāö

List<[BetaManagedAgentsMcpServerUrlDefinition](api/beta.md)> mcpServersÓāö

Model identifier and configuration.

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsModel idÓāö

The model that will power your agent.

See [models](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/models-overview) for additional details and options.

One of the following:

CLAUDE\_FABLE\_5("claude-fable-5")Óāö

Next generation of intelligence for the hardest knowledge work and coding problems

CLAUDE\_OPUS\_4\_8("claude-opus-4-8")Óāö

Frontier intelligence for long-running agents and coding

CLAUDE\_OPUS\_4\_7("claude-opus-4-7")Óāö

Frontier intelligence for long-running agents and coding

CLAUDE\_OPUS\_4\_6("claude-opus-4-6")Óāö

Most intelligent model for building agents and coding

CLAUDE\_SONNET\_4\_6("claude-sonnet-4-6")Óāö

Best combination of speed and intelligence

CLAUDE\_HAIKU\_4\_5("claude-haiku-4-5")Óāö

Fastest model with near-frontier intelligence

CLAUDE\_HAIKU\_4\_5\_20251001("claude-haiku-4-5-20251001")Óāö

Fastest model with near-frontier intelligence

CLAUDE\_OPUS\_4\_5("claude-opus-4-5")Óāö

Premium model combining maximum intelligence with practical performance

CLAUDE\_OPUS\_4\_5\_20251101("claude-opus-4-5-20251101")Óāö

Premium model combining maximum intelligence with practical performance

CLAUDE\_SONNET\_4\_5("claude-sonnet-4-5")Óāö

High-performance model for agents and coding

CLAUDE\_SONNET\_4\_5\_20250929("claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929")Óāö

High-performance model for agents and coding

ÓÄ™

Optional<Speed> speedÓāö

Inference speed mode. `fast` provides significantly faster output token generation at premium pricing. Not all models support `fast`; invalid combinations are rejected at create time.

One of the following:

STANDARD("standard")Óāö

FAST("fast")Óāö

class BetaManagedAgentsSessionStats:Óāö

Timing statistics for a session.

Optional<Double> activeSecondsÓāö

Cumulative time in seconds the session spent in running status. Excludes idle time.

Optional<Double> durationSecondsÓāö

Elapsed time since session creation in seconds. For terminated sessions, frozen at the final update.

class BetaManagedAgentsSessionUpdatedEvent:Óāö

Emitted when an UpdateSession request changed at least one field. Carries only the fields that changed; absent fields were not part of the update. The new configuration applies from the next turn.

String idÓāö

Unique identifier for this event.

LocalDateTime processedAtÓāö

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

Type typeÓāö

class BetaManagedAgentsSessionUsage:Óāö

Cumulative token usage for a session across all turns.

ÓÄ™

Optional<[BetaManagedAgentsCacheCreationUsage](api/beta.md)> cacheCreationÓāö

Prompt-cache creation token usage broken down by cache lifetime.

Optional<Long> ephemeral1hInputTokensÓāö

Tokens used to create 1-hour ephemeral cache entries.

Optional<Long> ephemeral5mInputTokensÓāö

Tokens used to create 5-minute ephemeral cache entries.

Optional<Long> cacheReadInputTokensÓāö

Total tokens read from prompt cache.

Optional<Long> inputTokensÓāö

Total input tokens consumed across all turns.

Optional<Long> outputTokensÓāö

Total output tokens generated across all turns.

ÓÄ™

class BetaManagedAgentsUserToolResultEvent:Óāö

Event sent by the client providing the result of an agent-toolset tool execution. Only valid on `self_hosted` environments, where sandbox-routed tools are executed by the client rather than the server.

String idÓāö

String toolUseIdÓāö

The id of the `agent.tool_use` event this result corresponds to, which can be found in the last `session.status_idle` [event's](api/beta/sessions/events/list.md) `stop_reason.event_ids` field.

Type typeÓāö

ÓÄ™

Optional<List<Content>> contentÓāö

The result content returned by the tool.

#### BetaSessionsEvents

##### [List Events](api/beta/sessions/events/list.md)

EventListPage beta().sessions().events().list(EventListParamsparams = EventListParams.none(), RequestOptionsrequestOptions = RequestOptions.none())

GET/v1/sessions/{session\_id}/events

##### [Send Events](api/beta/sessions/events/send.md)

[BetaManagedAgentsSendSessionEvents](api/beta.md) beta().sessions().events().send(EventSendParamsparams, RequestOptionsrequestOptions = RequestOptions.none())

POST/v1/sessions/{session\_id}/events

##### [Stream Events](api/beta/sessions/events/stream.md)

[BetaManagedAgentsStreamSessionEvents](api/beta.md) beta().sessions().events().streamStreaming(EventStreamParamsparams = EventStreamParams.none(), RequestOptionsrequestOptions = RequestOptions.none())

GET/v1/sessions/{session\_id}/events/stream

class BetaManagedAgentsAgentCustomToolUseEvent:Óāö

Event emitted when the agent calls a custom tool. The session goes idle until the client sends a `user.custom_tool_result` event with the result.

String idÓāö

Unique identifier for this event.

Input inputÓāö

Input parameters for the tool call.

String nameÓāö

Name of the custom tool being called.

LocalDateTime processedAtÓāö

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

Type typeÓāö

Optional<String> sessionThreadIdÓāö

When set, this event was cross-posted from a subagent's thread to surface its custom tool use on the primary thread's stream. Empty on the thread's own events. Echo this on a `user.custom_tool_result` event to route the result back.
